text:
(Specify any additional respondents)

(Specify if any adult party acts by a litigation friend)

(Specify if the children or any of them act by a children’s guardian)

Recitals

This order was made at a hearing at which both parties were present and represented.

(amend as appropriate)

The judge read the following [affidavits] / [witness statements] / [insert] and heard oral evidence from [name(s)].

The judge refused the application on the basis that the applicant had not established that [the [removal] / [retention] was in breach of the rights of custody of the applicant] / [the child[ren]] [was] / [were] habitually resident in [specify State]) immediately prior to the [removal] / [retention]].

The court records that, as: (a) the courts of [requesting State] have under consideration proceedings falling within the scope of the 1996 Hague Convention; and (b) which were commenced at a time that court had substantive jurisdiction pursuant to Articles 5-10, Article 13(1) of that Convention is engaged.

The court records that the courts of [requesting State] [have] / [have not] been seized of an application in matters of parental responsibility concerning the child[ren] and [are] / [are not] first seized of an application within the meaning of Article 16 of BIIR and that this court [is] / [is not] obliged to stay any proceedings concerning the child[ren] pursuant to Article 19(2) BIIR.

The orders in respect of the child[ren] are made pursuant to [Article 20 BIIR] / [Article 13 BIIR] / [Article 8 BIIR] / [Article 11 1996 Hague Convention] / [Article 12 1996 Hague Convention]/[Article 5 1996 Hague Convention].

[Insert any further recitals]

Undertakings to the court by the applicant [applicant name] or respondent [respondent name]

[Insert any undertakings (include an undertakings form in addition)]

IT IS ORDERED THAT:

The application for summary return pursuant to the 1980 Hague Convention is dismissed.

metadata.title: In the High Court of Justice
